pending.body:
finding_summary: `sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options` breaks terminal milestone sync for paused-then-resumed runs that keep the same `run_id`.
affected_files: `src/cli/run.rs`, `src/adapters/fs.rs`
line_references: `src/cli/run.rs:1174-1185`, `src/cli/run.rs:1277-1304`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:5065-5090`
details: On resume of a still-running attempt, `record_task_run_start` reuses the existing lineage row without moving `started_at`, so the lineage still points at the original start time. The new CLI sync path now prefers that stale lineage timestamp over the newer `run_resumed` boundary, then calls `terminal_event_for_attempt` against the pre-resume window. A normal pause -> resume -> complete/fail flow will therefore be rejected as "missing durable run_completed/run_failed", leaving milestone state unsynced even though the project journal is correct.
remediation_hint: Derive the current attempt boundary from the newest durable/snapshot resume boundary instead of letting a reused nonterminal lineage row override it, and add a regression test for pause -> resume -> complete/fail with the same running lineage row surviving across resume.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 14 -> Plan and Implement
